titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Brian Murray du Toit Collections
titlestmt.author: compiled by Teferi A. Adem and the staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday March 21, 2020 at 08:05
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.unittitle: Brian Murray du Toit collection
did.abstract: The collection consists of copies of speeches, public statements, press releases, memoranda, letters, church records, personal journals, travel documents, family photographs, kinship charts, field notes, manuscripts and related materials on the Inkatha Freedom Party and descendants of the Boers who left South Africa following the Anglo-Boer War of 1899-1902. The collection also includes materials on the Afrikaner Broederbond and the Dutch Reformed Church. Du Toit has used these materials in several published works, including three books on the Afrikaner diaspora: Colonial Boer: an Afrikaner Settlement in Chubut, Argentina (1995); Boer Settlers in the Southwest (1995); and The Boers in East Africa (1998).

langmaterial.language: Materials in this collection are in English, Afrikaans, Spanish, and German.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the African Collection Curator, 2004, 2007 and 2014.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Brian Murray du Toit Collection (MS 1839). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in four series and two additions: I. Buthelezi and the Inkatha Freedom Party, 1928-1989. II. Afrikaner Diaspora, 1902-1996. III. Brian M. du Toit Papers and Correspondence, 1903-1991. IV. Photographs, 1925-1933.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Brian Murray du Toit was born in Bloemfontein, South Africa, on March 2, 1935. He received a B.A. (1956) and an M.A. (1961) from the University of Pretoria and a Ph.D. (1963) from the University of Oregon. Beginning in 1966, du Toit taught anthropology at the University of Florida and conducted extensive research on various aspects of South Africa.

One of du Toit's research subjects has been the Boer (Afrikaner) who left South Africa following the Anglo-Boer war of 1899-1902. He studied Afrikaner diaspora settlements in Angola, East Africa (Kenya and Tanzania), Argentina and the American Southwest. Of Boer family background himself, du Toit conducted both archival and ethnographic research among descendants of Boer settlers in each of these countries. The outcome was a large collection of original materials including church records, personal journals, travel documents, and family photographs, genealogies and traditions. Du Toit has used these materials to publish several articles and three books.

Du Toit has also conducted research on prominent political parties in South Africa. For this reason, he corresponded with Chief Mangosuthu Gatsha Buthelezi, president of the Inkatha Freedom Party and former chief minister of KwaZulu. Du Toit also wrote on right wing white political parties in South Africa, including the former ruling National Party (NP) which has been identified with the Afrikaner people.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of copies of speeches, public statements, press releases, memoranda, letters, church records, personal journals, travel documents, family photographs, kinship charts, field notes, manuscripts and related materials on the Inkatha Freedom Party and descendants of the Boers who left South Africa following the Anglo-Boer War of 1899-1902. The collection also includes materials on the Afrikaner Broederbond and the Dutch Reformed Church. Du Toit has used these materials in several published works, including three books on the Afrikaner diaspora: Colonial Boer: an Afrikaner Settlement in Chubut, Argentina (1995); Boer Settlers in the Southwest (1995); and The Boers in East Africa (1998).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: Buthelezi and the Inkatha Freedom Party

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains copies of speeches, public statements and political/historical writings by Chief Mangosuthu Gatsha Buthelezi, president of the Inkatha Freedom Party (formerly Inkatha Yenkululeko Yesizwe, National Cultural Liberation) and chief minister of KwaZulu (1976-1994). The materials are arranged chronologically under two headings: Buthelezi, and Other documents on the Inkatha Freedom Party. Subjects covered in the materials include alliance among black political movements, debates over "peaceful" and "armed" struggle, socioeconomic well-being and access of black South Africans to education, etc. The speeches were delivered at different forums, including weekly church congregations, political rallies, round-table discussions, press conferences, and official government and party meetings. Public statements were released mainly through Inkatha's own Information Centre on South Africa and reflect Inkatha's official positions on a variety of South African issues during the struggle against the apartheid regime. Most of the materials were "EMBARGOED UNTIL DELIVERY" [in capital letters].

did.unittitle: Buthelezi, Mangosuthu Gatsha
"A short address to the African people of Bethelehem"

"White and black nationalism, ethnicity and the future of the homelands: can the homeland concept be the basis of a formula for a future South Africa?" [inaugural address to the Alfred and Winifred Hoernle memorial lecture, organized by the South African Institute of Race Relations]
"What it means to be a black teacher in South Africa" [an address to students at William Pitcher Teacher Training College]
"Speech at the opening of a wimpy bar at Mr. Winnington Sabela's new supermarket complex, Umlazi township"
"Detente in Southern Africa"
"Memorandum to the Prime Minister on the Urban Africans concerning recommendations to streamline existing procedures for urban Bantu and to amend legislation where necessary"

"A report back to the urban blacks of Umlazi on the conference black leaders had on urban blacks with the Hon. B. J. Vorster, Prime Minister of South Africa, held on the 22nd of January, 1975" [2 copies]
"A time for decision: an address to the Melmoth round table chairman's induction dinner"
"The launching of Inkatha at Kwamashu"
"Opening address" [to Inkatha's general conference]

"The Republican government's so-called final consolidation plans for KwaZulu, its economic implications and the black struggle for liberation" [address to the 1975 Natal Congress of the Chamber of Commerce]
"Words of appreciation at the first diploma-awarding ceremony to the first batch of graduates of the Institute for Industrial Workers"
"Address to members of the Mpumalanga regional authority and the people of Mpumalanga"
"The launching of Inkatha at Hlabisa"
"A brief address at the official opening of the New Germany, distillery of the Stellenbosch farmers wineries"
"Speech at the meeting held by the KwaZulu Executive Council with residents of Mondlo township"
"Speech at wilderness awareness evening"
"Meeting with General Wiseman, world leaders of the Salvation Army"
"Message to the conference of black leaders and the leadership of the Progressive Reform Party"
"The Anglican Church of Canada: the church and her role in the struggle for black liberation in South Africa" [an address to the National Executive of the Anglican Church of Canada]
"Correction: (" The World ") [reply to the newspaper's report on speech made by Buthelezi]
"Policy speech 1975 (committee stage)" [annual policy review speech to be discussed among committee members of the KwaZulu Assembly]
"In this approaching hour of crisis, a message to South Africa from black South Africa" [speech to Africans at the Jabulani Amphitheater in Soweto]
"Dialogue or confrontation?" [speech delivered at Kajuitraad, Afrikaner conference?]
"University education and the black struggle for liberation" [address on the occasion of the conferment of LL.D. by the University of Zululand, 2 copies]
"Policy speech (second reading)" [second draft? of chief minister's annual speech to the KwaZulu Assembly]
"Resolutions of the General Conference of Inkatha"
"Address at the inauguration of the regional branch office of in the Hlanganani regional area, Nxamalala Tribal Authority Office" [2 copies]
"Visit to riot-torn Soweto"
"Short address to Leo Marquard Hall annual dinner, University of Capetown"
"Toast proposal to the Capetown University debating union"
"The limitations of leadership in South Africa" [address to the University of Capetown]
"A viewpoint expressed to H. E. Dr. Henry Kissinger, the United States secretary of state"
"Presidential opening remarks to the Central Committee" [of Inkatha]
"Human rights and constitutional development in South Africa" [address to the Institute of International Affairs, Nigeria]
"My visit to Nigeria" [press release]
"Address to the Black Washington Technical Institute Convocation"
"Black Unity Front" [press statement by the steering committee for Black Alliance]
"Long Live the June 16th Movement" [submission to African Nationalists , a journal of the African National Congress, vol. 1, no. 1]
"Budget speech 1976 (committee stage)" [budget committee members of the KwaZulu Assembly]
"Corrections of week-end World report of my policy-speech"
"A short address at the opening of Rondavel classrooms of the Divine Life Society of South Africa"
"Opening address" [inaugural national congress of the Women's Brigade of the National Cultural Liberation Movement]
"Presidential address to the 3rd Ordinary Session of the [Inkatha] National Council" [theme: the employment of strength for liberation]
"Opening of Mr. Edward Ngobese's supermarket"
"Vote of thanks given at a banquet in honor of the chief minister and cabinet ministers of the KwaZulu government"
"Words of thanks to the Bilanyoni teaching fraternity, pupils and school committee"
"How can we become partners in the development of our people and in our liberation struggle"
"Meeting between Chief G. Buthelezi and Mr. J. T. Kruger, minister of justice, police and prisons" [press release]
"Policy speech: 1977" [outline of policies to be executed by the government of KwaZulu in the budget year]
"Address to Youth Brigade inaugural conference"
"Address to the Durban Rotary Club" [on the occasion of its celebration of 'International Understanding Week', 2 copies]
"South Africa in the 1980s" [address to the 30th anniversary congress of the South African Jewish Board of Deputies, 3 copies]
"Myths, fictions and politics" [address at the Graduate Institute of International Relations, Geneva, Switzerland]
"Statement in reply to Mr. R. F. "Pik" Botha, Minister of Foreign Affairs" [press release]
"A vote of thanks" [welcome speech on the visit of KwaZulu by the Hon. P. W. Botha, M. P., Prime Minister of South Africa and leader of the Nationalist Party]
"A black perspective of realism in the black struggle for liberation" [speech delivered at the annual rally at Jabulani amphitheater]
"Inkatha's evidence to the Commission of Inquiry on the Constitution" [memorandum]
"Rand Afrikaans University - Round table evening discussions: preliminary remarks" [2 copies]
"Why the alliance?" [speech at pro-South African black alliance mass solidarity rally]
"Address to KwaZulu Legislative Assembly prayer breakfast"
"The challenges of the black liberation struggle" [prayer meeting address to East Rand blacks]
"Presidential speech" [on the eve of the opening of the sixth ordinary general conference of Inkatha]
"Presidential address to the sixth ordinary general conference of Inkatha" [theme: 'Inkatha after 5 years']
Aide-mémoire for discussion with the German ambassador, His Excellency Dr. Eickhoff on his visit to Ulundi
"Speech delivered at the annual meeting and dinner of the Pinetown, New Germany Division Committee of the Natal Chamber of Industries"
"On relations between Inkatha and the leadership of the African National Congress mission in exile" [memorandum for discussion at Inkatha's Central Committee meeting] (2 copies)
"A rational for para-military developments within Inkatha" [paper presented for discussion by Central Committee]
"Education as an agency for change in the black liberation struggle" [speech delivered at the foundation stone laying ceremony of Mangosuthu Technikon]
"Speech made at the presentation of an Anglo-Zulu silver commemorative medal by the Italian ambassador"
"Memorandum to black leaders prior to meeting Mr. P. W. Botha"
"Memorandum to the Prime Minister of South Africa, the Hon. P. W. Botha"
"1980 Appropriation Bill" [policy speech read to KwaZulu Legislative Assembly]
"King Shaka day" [address to a public rally]
"Reaction to the State President's latest constitutional proposal"
Other documents on Inkatha
"Deed of trust and constitution of the Inkatha Ka Zulu (Zulu National Congress)"
Woord en Daad, no. 188 [news bulletin in Afrikaans]
Deurbraak [news bulletin in Afrikaans, vol. 7 no. 1-2]
"South Africa.... pathway to power or road to confrontation: Buthelezi's growing support in South Africa" [cover story in To the Point , Vol. 8, No.1]
"KwaZulu/Natal Indaba" [proposal to establish a multi-racial regional government]
"Mass meeting in South Africa" [topical briefing published by the Information Centre on South Africa]
"Inkatha's call for peace in South Africa" [topical briefing published by the Information Centre on South Africa]
Afrikaner diaspora

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of church records, personal journals, travel documents, family photographs, ethnographic notes, copies of news clippings and related materials concerning the Boers who settled in Argentina, Angola and East Africa (mainly Kenya and Tanzania) following the Anglo-Boer War of 1889-1902. Files are arranged alphabetically by country and chronologically within each country.

did.unittitle: Angola [Distrito do Uige reordenamento rural, in Portuguese, includes several photographs of schools, clinics, churches, other buildings, houses and water pumps]
Argentina
Boer emigration to Argentina [copies of documents on Boer leaders who originated and executed the plan to settle Boers there]

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Repatriation efforts [copies of newspaper clips, official correspondence, and related materials]

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Historisch overzicht der Argentijnsche Republic [by C. Ricchiardi, one of the organizers of Boer emigration to Argentina]

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

Konstitusie en reglement van orde van die Nederuits Geref. Vrouebond (Chubut, Argentinië)

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Die Algemene Armesorg, Ned. Geref. Kerk, K. P. [research notes, copies of bank documents and related materials on Afrikaners in Argentina]

altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Boerentrek naar Argentina [photocopies of news clippings, family photographs, and official documents]

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: East Africa [Kenya and Tanzania]
Oos-Afrika Vriendekomitee [business meeting minutes, correspondence, and photographs]
Oos-Afrika Vandag [extracts on Boers in Kenya, in Afrikaans]
Kerk op die Wit Hooglande
Danie Steyn [correspondence to different people, in Afrikaans]
Letter from Willy K. Kibet of Kenya to the Mina family [a South African Boer family in Kenya before 1963]
The last Afrikaner [story of Katerina Cornelia Odendaal, descendant of an Afrikaner family in Tanzania]
Rev. Abraham Chebiego to Rev. Piet Grobler [letter on personal and church issues, in English]
Die storie van 'n Afrika-plaas [a four pages extract from a journal, in Afrikaans]
Dagboek, 1933-1936, Ds. A. L. Aucamp [hand written in Afrikaans]
Kerk En Skool [in Afrikaans]
Nederduitse Gereformeerde Kerk Van Suid-Afrika, 1908-1958
Ned. Geref. Gemeente Monumentpark-Wes [includes photos of Boer families and black priests]
Brian M. du Toit Correspondence and Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files in this series are arranged chronologically and consist of correspondence, field notes and manuscripts. Most of the letters received by Professor du Toit are handwritten, except one which is typed on Inktha's official headed paper and personally signed by Chief G. M. Buthelezi. Some of the mails included copies of news extracts pertaining to Boer families.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, notes and copies of newspaper clippings on the Boers in Argentina

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, notes and copy of extracts on Boers who settled in New Mexico [South West USA]
Letters between Chief G. M. Buthelezi and Professor Brian M. du Toit
"The far right in current South African politics" [unpublished manuscript]
Research notes on the Boers in Argentina [includes several kinship charts of families during the "trek" and afterwards]
Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of photographs of Boer families in Argentina. It also includes a few photographs of Welsh families who settled in a neighboring community. The photographs are arranged alphabetically by family and personal name. Some photographs are unidentified. Researchers may be able to match these photographs with actual names of individuals and families by referring to Professor du Toit's 1995 book entitled Colonial Boer: an Afrikaner Settlement in Chubut, Argentina .

did.unittitle: Family/individual
Botha, Oomo Koos en Ds. Wasserfall
Conradie, Francois D. [portrait photograph and a brief letter to du Toit from a descendant]
De San Martin, General Jose [photograph of a settlement in the background]
Heirdi [family] fotos kan u hou
Huisie, Steen in Serra Chaira [includes a photograph entitled "Disngaansfees in die Rio Chico 16 Des. 1926" in the back]
Jacobs, J. A. ["Eerste kerkraadsvergadering in 1907 deur Ds J. A. Jacobs ter Stigting van die N. G. Gemeente"
Kruger family
Kruger family [several photographs with names of members of the Kruger family written on the back]
Kruger, Piet and family
Marie Magdelina Kruger [birth certificate, childhood photographs and family portrait]
Reprints [mostly duplicate copies of the families of Kruger and Viviers]
Myburg family and Nellie Schoemen
Pienaar, A, Visser, M. G. G. and Conradie, F. D. [die eerste bybels in Argentinie]
Riviers family and other Boers in Comodoro Rivadavia [includes several photographs, negatives, handwritten notes and copies of news extracts]
Viviers family
Family photographs [with names of members of the Viviers family]
Viviers, Wasserfall and Venter [group photographs of family members]
Johannes van der Walt, Mev. Henning, Mev. Viviers, kinders en klien kinders [group photograph]
Weber, Joseph and family
Other families
First generation Boer families who settled in Argentina [copies of extract photos from newspapers and magazines]
Unidentified families [includes 2 different portraits of one family and birthday cake and celebration for an elderly woman from another family]
Unidentified Welsh families in Argentina
Additional Material
Research files
Flierl, Leonhard
Correspondence, in German
Unter Wilden: Missionarische Anfangsarbeit im Innern von Neuguinea , von Leonhard Flierl
Wilson, Monica Hunter
Materials, primarily reprints, by and about anthropologist Monica Wilson
Additional Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession includes research materials and photographs documenting the Afrikaner Broederbond, an isolated Afrikaner community in the Gamkaskloof Valley; Zulu folk narratives; and hallucinogenic drug use in Southern Africa and South America.

did.unittitle: Afrikaner Broederbond
Gamkaskloof background
Gamkaskloof photos
Gamkaskloof school children
Gamkaskloof "Draw-A-Man"
Gamkaskloof, Cordiers genealogy
Gamkaskloof genealogies from the University of Cape Town
Hallucinogenic drug use photos, Southern Africa and South America
Zulu folk narratives
